digest.top_line: KEYNOTE-689 (perioperative pembro, EFS+) and NIVOPOSTOP (postop nivo, 3-yr DFS ~63 vs 53%): two positive phase III IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC on an unchanged surgery+CRT backbone.
digest.tldr: Head/neck carried the day: two positive phase III IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC differ in agent and timing (perioperative pembro vs postop nivo) but share an unchanged surgery + adjuvant CRT backbone. RT dose/volume/fractionation are untouched; IO is the new add-on. Source is a secondary infographic, so HRs/CIs and KEYNOTE-689 EFS magnitude aren't quantified.

sites.intro: Both trials hold the surgery + adjuvant CRT backbone constant; the new variable is the IO agent and its timing, not RT dose, volume, or fractionation.

studies.name: KEYNOTE-689 vs NIVOPOSTOP
studies.tldr: Two positive phase III IO trials in resectable LA-HNSCC: perioperative pembro (EFS+) vs postoperative nivo (3yr DFS ~63 vs 53%), both layered on surgery+adjuvant CRT.

details.text: 📊 KEYNOTE-689 vs NIVOPOSTOP — design + headline (per infographic)

details: 🔍 RT/CRT backbone is unchanged in both: the new variable is the IO agent and its timing, not RT dose, volume, or fractionation
⚠️ No RT technique parameters (dose, fractionation, target volume) reported in source; transferability to your plan can't be judged from this infographic
🔍 KEYNOTE-689 schema: 2 cycles neoadjuvant pembro → surgery → adjuvant RT/CRT + pembro → maintenance pembro
🔍 NIVOPOSTOP (GORTEC 2018-01): high-risk resected → post-op cisplatin CRT ± nivo; high-risk = +margin, ENE, ≥4 nodes, extensive PNI
📊 KEYNOTE-689 positive for EFS; called first positive perioperative IO trial in resectable HNSCC in >2 decades. Effect size (HR/CI/median) not reported in source
🔗 PD-L1 CPS: higher CPS appears to derive greater benefit (KEYNOTE-689, per infographic)
⚠️ Source is a secondary educational infographic, not primary trial data; HRs, CIs, and KEYNOTE-689 EFS magnitude not quantified
open_questions: Perioperative pembrolizumab vs postoperative nivolumab: optimal IO timing untested head-to-head
PD-L1 CPS threshold for selecting IO benefit in resected HNSCC
Whether adding IO changes adjuvant RT dose, volume, or fractionation

related_trials.brief_title: Phase II Multi-centered Study of Perioperative Ivonescimab Versus Pembrolizumab Combined with Standard of Care (SOC) in Patients with Resectable, Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
related_trials.brief_summary:
Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC) is one of the common malignant tumors in the world. More than 60% of HNSCC patients are locally advanced when first diagnosed. The treatment effect of locally advanced HNSCC is not ideal. About 50-60% of patients will have local recurrence within 2 years, and 20-30% of patients will have distant metastasis. The 5-year disease control rate is approximately 40%, and the 5-year overall survival rate is less than 50%.

In recent years, PD-1 inhibitors…
conditions: Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma HNSCC

related_trials.brief_title: Sintilimab Plus Chemotherapy as Neoadjuvant and Adjuvant Treatment for Locally Advanced Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma
related_trials.brief_summary:
This is a multicenter, open-label, randomized phase III clinical trial evaluating perioperative treatment with sintilimab combined with chemotherapy in patients with locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma.

Despite standard treatment with surgery followed by postoperative radiotherapy or chemoradiotherapy, patients with locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma remain at high risk of recurrence or metastasis. Recent evidence, including results from the KEYNOTE-689 study, suggests…
conditions: Locally Advanced Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma

interventions.name: Sintilimab plus nab-paclitaxel and platinum-based chemotherapy
Radical surgery combined with radiotherapy or chemoradiotherapy

related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Aged 18 to 75 years at the time of enrollment.
* ECOG Performance Status (PS) score of 0-1.
* Primary lesion pathologically confirmed as oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC), including tumors of the anterior two-thirds of the tongue, gingiva, buccal mucosa, floor of the mouth,…

For patients who have already undergone surgery and

to postoperative CRT improves outcomes compared
with CRT alone.

(higher CPS appears to derive greater benefit)
